questions.question: What is the purpose of using the 'super' keyword in a child class?
questions.hint: It calls the constructor of the parent class.

questions.question: What happens when you create a multilevel hierarchy using inheritance, and a child class has a constructor that calls the parent class's constructor?
questions.hint: The child class's object cannot be created successfully.

questions.question: What happens if you do not call the parent class's constructor from a child class's constructor?
questions.hint: The child class's object will not have the same state as the parent class's object.
